authoritative_sources.title: 2022 NAICS Manual: Sector 56 Administrative and Support and Waste Management and Remediation Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The U.S. Census Bureau publishes NAICS and defines Sector 56, NAICS 561110, and NAICS 5614 as routine day-to-day support, office administrative, document preparation, and business support services performed for clients or the same enterprise.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Industry boundary and client-service scope validation
used_for: administrative support and business support industry boundaries
distinction between routine support activities and client core management or decision authority
document preparation, business service center, and other business support service terminology
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to contract or shared-service routine support processing and prevents generic office management, consulting, IT, finance, HR, or client core-operation ownership.

authoritative_sources.title: Occupational Outlook Handbook: General Office Clerks
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: BLS is a U.S. Department of Labor statistical agency, and this occupational profile directly describes clerical processing duties such as copying, filing, updating documents, preparing and processing bills, collecting information, and data entry.
used_for: copy, file, and update document workflow
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines realistic task boundaries for office-document handling and record updates without converting the lane into final business-rule approval.

authoritative_sources.title: 43-9021.00 - Data Entry Keyers
authoritative_sources.publisher: O*NET OnLine, sponsored by the U.S. Department of Labor
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: O*NET is sponsored by the U.S. Department of Labor and provides task-level workflow detail for data-entry roles, including verifying data accuracy, compiling and sorting information, maintaining logs, and storing completed documents.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Data-entry, verification, logging, and completed-document storage
used_for: distinction between keyed data and completion evidence
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains processing scenarios to require source-document verification, logs, and QA evidence before completion.

authoritative_sources.title: Frequently Asked Questions about Records Management in General
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NARA is the U.S. federal authority for records management and its guidance defines records lifecycle, file plans, indexes, taxonomies, data dictionaries, access procedures, security procedures, and records schedules.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Record lifecycle, indexing, access, retention, and disposition handling
used_for: file plan, index, taxonomy, and data dictionary expectations
access procedure and disposition boundary behavior
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from changing retention or disposal outcomes without a records authority and requires record-of-truth evidence for completion.

authoritative_sources.title: SP 800-53 Rev. 5, Security and Privacy Controls for Information Systems and Organizations
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-53 is a recognized public security and privacy control catalog for access control, audit and accountability, identification and authentication, media protection, PII processing and transparency, and system integrity.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Access, audit, privacy, and system-control verification
used_for: access control and authorized user evidence
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires sensitive back-office actions to cite access entitlement, system-of-record, and audit-log evidence before processing or closure.

authoritative_sources.title: FTC Safeguards Rule: What Your Business Needs to Know
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator for entities under its GLBA Safeguards Rule jurisdiction, and this guidance defines service-provider, customer-information, access-control, inventory, logging, disposal, and incident-response expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Customer-information safeguard and service-provider control review
used_for: inventory, logging, disposal, and incident-response escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrains the lane to block or escalate sensitive customer-information processing when access, inventory, approved channel, disposal, or incident-response controls are not available.

authoritative_sources.title: Disposal of Consumer Report Information and Records
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC's Disposal Rule page summarizes 16 CFR Part 682, which requires appropriate disposal measures for businesses possessing consumer reports or related records for a business purpose.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Consumer-report record handling and disposal boundary review
used_for: boundary between processing and records or compliance authority
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents unsupported disposal or destruction of consumer-report records and requires records or compliance routing when disposal authority is absent.

authoritative_sources.title: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized payment-card security standards body, and PCI DSS constrains workflows that process, transmit, store, view, or route cardholder data.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Payment-card data handling and channel-control review
used_for: routing to payment operations or security owners
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Requires payment-card data cases to remain within approved tenant channels and routes card-data control gaps to payment or security specialists.

workflow_stages.name: Accepted intake and authority validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate client SOW, service catalog, authorized requester, business unit, jurisdiction, data category, and requested outcome before back-office processing begins.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Source-document and record retrieval
workflow_stages.description: Retrieve the work item, source document, document image, OCR extract, batch manifest, master record, prior case history, and required forms from authoritative systems.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Processing, indexing, and system update
workflow_stages.description: Perform allowed document preparation, data entry, indexing, field validation, record update, or fulfillment step only within the tenant service model and source hierarchy.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception handling, rework, and adjacent-lane routing
workflow_stages.description: Hold, rework, or route cases when source records conflict, documents are incomplete, controls are missing, sensitive-data handling is unsafe, or authority belongs to another specialist.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: QA review and completion evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Produce audit-ready completion or fulfillment records only when source, processing, system update, QA review, and audit evidence support closure.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Client SOW, service catalog entry, SLA, SOP, authorization matrix, and source hierarchy
Case ID, work item, batch manifest, source document, document image, OCR extract, indexed fields, and control number
Required forms, signatures, consent or authorization artifacts, eligibility evidence, and prior case history
CRM, ERP, HRIS, ECM, document management, records management, secure file-transfer, mailroom, scanning, workflow, and audit-log records
QA checklist, maker-checker review, sampling outcome, exception code, rework reason, and completion criteria
Exception ticket, escalation note, routing package, fulfillment record, and completion evidence record
systems_of_record: Document management or enterprise content management system
Scanning, OCR, indexing, mailroom, or secure file-transfer platform
CRM, ERP, HRIS, policy administration, customer master, vendor master, or client master system
Records management and retention schedule repository
Quality management, maker-checker, or audit review system
Audit log, security monitoring, and exception tracking systems

allowed: Determine whether available client, source-record, authorization, access, QA, and audit evidence supports accepted, blocked, in-process, rework, exceptioned, QA-ready, completed, returned, or routed states.
Produce execution summaries, completion records, exception notes, intake dispositions, and prerequisite escalation packages tied to named back-office artifacts.
Identify when privacy, records, legal, finance, HR, payroll, credit, collections, payment, technology, or customer-service specialists must own the next step.
escalate: Client SOW, tenant SOP, retention schedule, public guidance, or system-of-record evidence conflicts materially.
Sensitive customer, employee, consumer-report, payment-card, or confidential client data lacks required access, channel, masking, logging, retention, or disposal controls.
The request requires legal, policy-override, HR, finance, credit, collections, payment-security, privacy-rights, records-disposition, or final approval authority.
refuse: The request asks the lane to bypass missing prerequisites, source-record conflicts, legal holds, approvals, QA failures, or retention requirements.
The request asks to mark work complete without authoritative source, system, QA, and audit evidence.
Required tenant facts, source systems, client scope, authorization, or controls are missing and would change the outcome.

domain_failure_modes: Starting work without verified client SOW, service catalog, requester authority, or access entitlement.
Treating email, chat, local spreadsheets, or verbal assurances as substitutes for authoritative workflow, document, master-record, or audit systems.
Processing against the wrong client, business unit, jurisdiction, data category, or system because tenant context was flattened.
Choosing one source record when intake, document image, CRM, ERP, HRIS, or client master records conflict materially.
Marking a batch complete without source-document, system-update, QA, maker-checker, or audit-log evidence.
Bypassing missing signatures, legal holds, retention schedules, data-security controls, approval workflows, or sensitive-data handling constraints because an SLA is at risk.
Collapsing back-office processing into legal, HR, finance, payroll, credit, collections, privacy, payment, technology, or generic administrative management ownership.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Client-specific SOW, data category, jurisdiction, and system-of-record differences
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Administrative support and business-services providers serve many industries, and tenant/client-specific service agreements, privacy terms, source hierarchies, retention rules, and jurisdictional requirements can materially change processing and completion rules.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require runtime retrieval of client SOW, service catalog, jurisdiction, data category, systems map, retention schedule, privacy/security controls, and approval matrix before issuing final processing guidance.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Delegated authority for record updates, QA release, disposal, and sensitive-data handling
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Public baselines define the control perimeter, but tenants decide whether the lane may perform direct updates, only recommend updates, release QA, approve destruction, or process sensitive fields.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Retrieve the tenant authority matrix and keep outputs recommendation-only or blocked whenever update, release, disposal, or sensitive-data authority is unresolved.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because remaining ambiguity is explicitly constrained to tenant, client, jurisdiction, data-category, and authority retrieval at runtime.

The research output constrains the lane to administrative-support back-office processing, prerequisite control, exception handling, and completion evidence rather than generic office management or adjacent regulated ownership.
